title: lantana macropodioides
intro: Lantana macropodioides, also known as the large buttonwood or West Indian shrub, is a species of flowering plant in the verbena family, native to the Caribbean and parts of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Lantana macropodioides have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential use in treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a natural insect repellent. Lantana macropodioides is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
